$comment: Single source for the generated README sections. Per-skill summaries live in each skill.json (metadata.catalog_summary); this file holds family-level prose and the routing matrix. Rendered by scripts/build-catalog.py into README.md between BEGIN/END GENERATED markers. Edit here, then run `python3 scripts/build-catalog.py --write`.

pick_a_skill.intro: Two questions get you there: **which surface**, and are you **reviewing it** or **building it**?

rows.surface: **Developer experience** — APIs, SDKs, CLIs, setup, errors, auth, packaging, IDE, plugins, telemetry
**Documentation** — READMEs, CHANGELOGs, quickstarts, API refs, contributor onboarding, samples, help centers, OpenAPI/MCP tool contracts
**Writing** — memos/PRDs/RFCs, technical & task docs, talks/pitches, narratives, general prose
**Product UX & accessibility** — usability, forms, navigation/IA, error/recovery, WCAG
**Visual UI craft** — dashboards, design systems/tokens, prototypes, motion, decks, handoff
**Artifact ↔ host integration** — embeddable HTML artifacts: postMessage / persistence / fixed-canvas / direct-edit / export contract with an editing shell
**Minimal, modular code** — slop, duplication, over-engineering; dependency direction, deep modules; right-sizing structure so many agents can work in parallel

rows.need: **Source-cited research** — an open-ended topic report, or validating a named opportunity to a go/no-go decision
**Agent as developer** — the SDK / tool / error / telemetry surface an AI agent consumes
**Agent as reader** — AGENTS.md, llms.txt, tool descriptions, machine-readable reference for agents
**Agent as end-user** — an agent-operable UI / app / computer-use surface
**Agent as operator** — observability, trace-and-eval loops, autonomy, reliability
**Agent as subject** — evals, LLM-as-judge, benchmarks, activation tests
↳ harden a repo for coding agents (AGENTS.md, hooks, gates, sandbox)
↳ turn observed agent failures into closure work, rules, gates, or skill/eval fixes
**Second opinion from Codex** — drive the Codex CLI to review code/docs, give an independent take, or reflect across projects
**Second opinion from Claude Code** — review working-tree/branch changes, a second opinion, cross-project reflection, or a hosted ultrareview
**Second opinion from Cursor** — review or analyze under a *different* model (gpt-5, sonnet-4, …) via `cursor-agent`
**Trim your agent's context budget** — audit idle MCP servers and unused plugins/skills/commands/subagents by per-session token cost, then prune safely

unsure: **Still unsure?** The two boundaries people hit most:

- *"Make our docs better"* — audit existing docs → `docs-audit`; reshape docs IA → `docs-design`; API/SDK friction beyond the docs → `dx-audit`; agent-native docs (llms.txt, AGENTS.md, retrieval) → `agent-docs`.

- *"Improve our agent"* — make a coding-agent harness work in this repo (AGENTS.md, hooks, MCP) → `harden-repo-for-coding-agents`; design evals/benchmarks for an AI product → `agent-test`; operate its trace-and-eval loop, autonomy, and reliability → `agent-ops`.

catalog.lead_in: Every skill, grouped by family: what it does and what it's grounded in. To route by task, see [Pick a skill](./README.md#pick-a-skill); to install, see [Install](./README.md#install).

families.title: Heuristic audit & design pairs
intro: One pair per surface: **`-audit`** reviews an existing surface and produces a severity-scored findings report; **`-design`** shapes a new one and produces a design doc, plan, or runbook. Grounded in 120+ cited sources — Norman, Nielsen, Bloch, Gregg, the Google SRE book, Kleppmann, WCAG 2.2, Ousterhout — with per-skill provenance in each skill's `skill.json`.

families.title: Agent experience (AX)
intro: Each human-experience domain gets an agent-facing counterpart, organized by the role the agent plays: `agent-dx` (agent as developer), `agent-docs` (agent as reader), `agent-ux` (agent as end-user), `agent-ops` (agent as operator), and `agent-test` (agent as the thing being measured) — plus the two hands-on skills they route to, `harden-repo-for-coding-agents` and `rules-from-coding-agent-failures`.

families.title: Cross-agent interop & tooling
intro: Drive *another* coding agent's CLI as an independent second-opinion reviewer or analysis pass. All three default to read-only and present their output as an external opinion to reconcile against local evidence — useful when you want a different model or provider to check work before you ship it.

families.title: Context budget & agent-setup hygiene
intro: Measure and reclaim the context tokens your agent setup burns every session — the MCP servers, plugins, skills, slash commands, and subagents that load whether or not you use them.
